name: prisma
summary: Next-generation Node.js and TypeScript ORM with type-safe queries, migrations, and a visual schema editor.

story.problem: Writing raw SQL is tedious and error-prone. Traditional ORMs like Sequelize give you objects but lose TypeScript types as soon as you query. You end up casting `any` everywhere and discovering schema mismatches at runtime.
story.mental_model: Prisma has three parts: Schema (define models in schema.prisma), Migrate (generate SQL migrations from schema diffs), and Client (auto-generated, fully type-safe query builder). When you run `prisma generate`, it reads your schema and generates a `PrismaClient` with exact types for every model and query.
story.when_to_use: TypeScript backends with relational DBs (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, SQL Server). Projects where type safety and DX matter. Next.js full-stack apps.
story.when_not_to_use: If you need very complex SQL that Prisma can't express — you can fall back to $queryRaw. Pure NoSQL (use Mongoose for MongoDB). Extreme performance-critical paths (Prisma adds overhead vs raw SQL).

alternatives.name: drizzle-orm
alternatives.reason: Lighter, SQL-first, no code generation. Faster queries, smaller bundle, growing fast.

alternatives.name: typeorm
alternatives.reason: Decorator-based ORM, similar to Java Hibernate. More mature, more complex.

alternatives.name: kysely
alternatives.reason: Type-safe SQL query builder without ORM abstractions. Full SQL control with types.

alternatives.name: sequelize
alternatives.reason: Older, battle-tested ORM. Less TypeScript-friendly but has a huge ecosystem.

nodes.label: prisma
nodes.summary: CLI tool: `npx prisma generate`, `prisma migrate dev`, `prisma studio`.

nodes.label: schema.prisma
nodes.summary: Declarative schema file: datasource (DB connection), generator (client config), model (table definitions).

nodes.label: PrismaClient
nodes.summary: Auto-generated type-safe client. `const prisma = new PrismaClient()`. One instance per app.

nodes.label: find* queries
nodes.summary: prisma.user.findUnique(), findFirst(), findMany(). Accept where, select, include, orderBy, skip, take.

nodes.label: Write operations
nodes.summary: create(), update(), upsert(), delete(), createMany(), updateMany(), deleteMany().

nodes.label: Relations & include
nodes.summary: Define 1:1, 1:N, N:M in schema. Query with `include: { posts: true }` for eager loading.

nodes.label: prisma migrate dev
nodes.summary: Compares schema to DB, generates SQL migration file, applies it. Tracks migration history.

nodes.label: Prisma Studio
nodes.summary: `npx prisma studio` — visual DB browser in the browser. Browse, filter, edit records.

nodes.label: $transaction
nodes.summary: prisma.$transaction([...ops]) for sequential atomic ops. Or callback form for interactive transactions.

steps.label: Install
steps.code:
npm install prisma @prisma/client
npx prisma init

steps.label: Schema
Setup
steps.code:
import { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client'
const prisma = new PrismaClient()

steps.label: Create
Read
Update
Schema relation
Query with include
